---
title: "Sahil Imtiyaz"
subtitle: "Mathematical structures for evolving biological and complex systems"
about:
  template: jolla
  image: image.png
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:sahil.imtiyaz@unicam.it
    - icon: google
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=YOUR-SCHOLAR-ID
    - icon: file-earmark-person
      text: CV
      href: cv.qmd
---

::: {.hero-box}
I develop mathematical frameworks for understanding how structure, dynamics, and constraints co-evolve in biological, physical, and communication systems.

My work is motivated by local-to-global consistency problems, multi-level biological organization, evolving network architectures, and the role of interfaces in shaping feasible system behavior.
:::

## Research identity

My research sits at the intersection of **mathematical biology**, **complex systems**, **applied topology**, **network science**, and **theoretical/computational modeling**.

I am especially interested in systems where the underlying state space, interaction structure, and admissible dynamics are not fixed once and for all, but evolve together.

Key themes include:

- constraint semantics for multi-level biological organization;
- evolving simplicial and network-bundle frameworks;
- local-to-global compatibility, extension, and feasibility problems;
- interface-mediated organization and circular causality diagnostics;
- applications to molecular communication, Hodgkin-type signalling loops, viral release, and evolving communication networks.

## Current focus

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card}
### Multi-level organization

Mathematical semantics for biological systems with heterogeneous levels, active interfaces, and constrained realizability.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card}
### Evolving networks

Geometric and topological tools for network reconfiguration, feasibility, and interface-loop diagnostics.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card}
### Local-global structure

Obstruction, holonomy, and compatibility methods for detecting when local admissibility fails to produce global realizability.
:::
:::

:::

## Selected links

- [Research overview](research.qmd)
- [Publications and manuscripts](publications.qmd)
- [Projects](projects.qmd)
- [CV](cv.qmd)
- [Contact](contact.qmd)
